evidence.claim: VESA DisplayHDR specification defines standardized peak brightness measurement protocols (10% window, 30-minute sustained); VESA DisplayHDR 400/600/1000/1400 tiers correspond to certified peak luminance values.
RTINGS independent motion-handling tests document the difference between true panel refresh and marketing 'motion rate' figures.
HDR peak brightness claims are short-duration peak (typically 10% window); sustained full-screen brightness is 30-60% of the peak figure.

criteria.name: panel_type
criteria.notes: OLED wins on black levels; Mini-LED wins on brightness; QLED is mid-range.

criteria.name: refresh_rate_hz
criteria.notes: True panel refresh, not marketing 'motion rate' figures (E1).

criteria.name: input_lag_ms
criteria.notes: Critical for gaming; <20ms is excellent, >40ms is laggy.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Marketing figure, not true panel refresh (E1). Panel is 60 or 120Hz; motion interpolation is a separate feature.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cited Hz figure the true panel refresh rate or a marketing 'motion rate' metric?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Peak brightness is short-duration (10% window); sustained full-screen brightness is far lower (E2). Real-world HDR viewing uses the sustained figure.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the brightness figure specify peak-vs-sustained and window size?

confabulationPatterns.pattern: smart TV with access to 10,000+ apps
confabulationPatterns.reality: App count includes redundant versions, low-quality apps, and inactive bloat. Relevant metric is support for the specific apps the user uses (Netflix, YouTube, Max, Disney+, etc.).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the TV support the specific streaming services the user actually uses?

compatibilityQuestions: Room lighting: bright/sunlit room favors high-brightness Mini-LED over OLED.
Primary use: movies (favor OLED black levels), gaming (favor low input lag + 120Hz), sports (favor brightness + motion).

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Soundbar or external audio (modern thin TVs have poor speakers)

repairability.notes: Panel replacement typically costs more than a new TV. Power boards and control boards can be repaired by specialty shops.
